title: Adopt the governed source registry as a required Toolkit control
context: The Toolkit requires explicit authority, provenance, freshness, and eligibility controls before evidence can support consequential outputs.

decision.statement: Require governed source records for all evidence used in final decision outputs.
decision.rationale: The source registry directly reduces stale-evidence and unsupported-claim risk while providing reusable audit data for every workflow.

alternatives.title: Adopt the governed registry
alternatives.rationale: Provides explicit controls with low operating cost and broad reuse.

alternatives.title: Rely on chat history and model memory
alternatives.rationale: Does not provide durable freshness, authority, or decision-eligibility evidence.

evidence.claim: A durable source registry is required to prevent long-running work from silently degrading as sources age.

assumptions.statement: Structured source records can be introduced before the persistence layer is complete.

risks.description: Manual registration may slow low-risk work.
risks.mitigation: Use minimal required fields and automate registration after schema stabilization.

conditions: Expired or unknown sources cannot support final decisions
confidence_basis: The control responds directly to documented failure modes and passed structural regression tests.

triggers: A stale source reaches a final output
